title: The Necessity of Including China in Global AI Governance
content:
For a long time, Western tech giants believed they could dominate the global artificial intelligence landscape without worrying about foreign competition. However, at the start of last year, the inadequacy of this assumption was starkly exposed. A Chinese lab unveiled a powerful AI system, shattering the delusion that China could be left out of global AI governance. Despite an American-led effort to deprive China of the most powerful computer chips, the new model was almost as close to Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) as its Western rivals. Other Chinese tech firms soon rolled out similarly capable models, changing the geopolitical tech landscape completely.

Experts quickly spelled out the severe consequences of this unforeseen setback. They argued that any international safety collaboration that completely excluded China would be entirely pointless. If even one or two of these isolated projects design harmful AGIs, it could pose an existential threat to humanity. A lasting safety regime cannot be spearheaded exclusively by private Western companies or strictly dictated by US government policies. Ultimately, true global AI security requires respecting the brief history of the technology and acknowledging that it cannot safely ignore China.
